steps.title: Use the northern-site clue
steps.content: The finding matters because there had been a lack of known nesting sites in northern South America. That gap led paleontologists to assume titanosaurs migrated south to lay eggs.

steps.title: Avoid unsupported inferences
steps.content: A claims it is the earliest known site, which the text does not say. B invents excavation difficulty. C adds other sauropods, but the evidence is specifically about titanosaurs. The answer is D.
